topic: 现有框架下，fabric cli和 mcp_server 优化支持的相关命令(增删改查)，以及相应命令的功能和职责划分和优化。
key_findings: CLI is the broad control plane and should own high-impact local writes.
MCP server currently exposes two read-only cognitive tools plus two deprecated compatibility write tools.
HTTP server is a multi-protocol host: REST APIs, SSE, Dashboard, and MCP HTTP transport.
sync-meta is the canonical registry update path because it derives meta from source rule files.
scan has duplicated CLI and HTTP implementations and should be service-shared.
Deprecated MCP write tools should not be expanded into new CRUD surface.
discussion_points: Should low-level commands such as config/bootstrap/hooks remain public, or be documented as advanced plumbing under init/update?
Should scan/doctor status be exposed to MCP as read-only tools/resources, or remain CLI/HTTP only?
What compatibility window should apply to fab_update_registry and fab_append_intent?
open_questions: Whether to hide deprecated MCP write tools from common workflows before full removal.
Whether doctor --fix and sync-meta need clearer semantic separation.
Where to locate the shared scan service: @fenglimg/fabric-shared/node or @fenglimg/fabric-server service exported to CLI.

findings: MCP ToolAnnotations are hints, not hard enforcement.
MCP resources are intended for context and support change notifications.
Tools can return resource links and should be treated as model-invoked actions.
best_practices: Keep destructive local mutations in explicit CLI workflows.
Use resources or read-only tools for runtime context.
codebase_gaps: scan report generation is not shared.
deprecated write tools remain visible in MCP server.
